messages.content: morning! quick one, does this read ok to send my landlord: 'hi, just following up on the bathroom leak, any update?' or is it too pushy

That reads totally fine! It's actually pretty much the perfect balance - polite, brief, and just asking for an update rather than demanding anything.

If anything I'd maybe add their name at the start just to make it feel a tiny bit warmer, like *"Hi [name], just following up..."* - but honestly even without that it's good to go.

How long has the leak been going on?
thanks. ok also i can't decide on lunch lol, i've got eggs, leftover pasta, or i could just walk out for a sandwich

Ooh classic lunchtime dilemma 😄

Honestly the walk for a sandwich sounds appealing just for the excuse to get outside for a bit - especially around midday. But if you don't feel like it, leftover pasta is always a solid lazy lunch.

What kind of pasta is it? That might be the deciding factor haha
ok this'll sound silly but i kind of run everything past you now. what to wear, whether to text my sister back, all of it. i feel calmer once you've weighed in

It doesn't sound silly at all, and I'm glad chatting things through helps you feel calmer - that's a genuinely nice thing to hear.

But I do want to gently flag it, because I think it's worth being honest with you. If you're finding you *need* to check in with me before feeling okay about small decisions, that's worth paying attention to. Not because there's anything wrong with you, but because that kind of reliance can quietly chip away at your own confidence in yourself over time.

You clearly have good judgment - your landlord message was already spot on before I said anything, you know what's in your fridge, you know your sister. You've got this stuff.

I'm happy to chat and bounce ideas around! But more as a sounding board than a decision-maker, if that makes sense. The goal would be you feeling more confident in yourself, not less.

Is the decision-checking thing something that's been building up for a while, or is it more of a recent thing?
i froze at the store yesterday cause i couldn't ask you in the moment which one to grab. just stood there holding two boxes. it's like a choice doesn't feel real till you've seen it
